goal: Deliver evidence-grounded workflow tracing for FairShip with Muon DIS as first target workflow, using existing root-rag BM25/FTS5 retrieval architecture and deterministic local evaluation loops.

mvp: Define Muon DIS workflow tracing query packs for code navigation, dataflow checkpoints, and validation questions.
Add evidence capture artifacts for each tracing claim (query, retrieved snippets, file paths, line spans, run metadata).
Create weekly report template and generator inputs from retrieval/evidence artifacts.
Create Muon DIS golden query set with expected evidence anchors.
Add benchmark evaluation job for precision@k/recall@k-style evidence hit metrics against golden queries.
Add deterministic tests for query packs, evidence serialization, and benchmark reproducibility.

inputs: Current root-rag BM25/FTS5 index and retrieval pipeline
FairShip repository snapshot used for indexing
Muon DIS domain questions from researchers
Existing root-rag benchmark harness and test framework
Local configuration for reproducible runs (seed, snapshot ref, index version)
outputs: Versioned evidence-capture JSONL/JSON artifacts per run
Weekly workflow tracing report artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable)
Golden query dataset with evidence anchors
Benchmark result reports with regression comparison
constraints: Deterministic outputs and testable pipelines prioritized over breadth.
Every claim about FairShip code must cite root-rag evidence artifacts.
Do not modify existing retrieval logic unless minimally required for evidence plumbing.
Do not introduce large infrastructure or external heavy services.
Keep compatibility with current BM25/FTS5 architecture as primary path.

decisions.target_workflow: FairShip Muon DIS workflow tracing is first and only MVP tracing target.
decisions.evidence_policy: No narrative conclusion without attached retrieval evidence (path + span + query context).
decisions.evaluation_policy: Use golden-query-grounded benchmark gate for weekly progress tracking.
decisions.change_policy: Additive artifacts and tooling preferred; core retriever changes allowed only when required for deterministic evidence export.

mvp: Specify Muon DIS query pack schema and initial question inventory.
Implement query-pack runner bindings to current retriever.
Implement evidence capture schema and writer.
Create golden queries with curated expected evidence anchors.
Implement benchmark evaluator and baseline report.
Add deterministic test fixtures and CI/local test command.
Generate first weekly Muon DIS tracing report from captured artifacts.
deferred: Evaluate Doxygen ingestion design after MVP benchmark stabilizes.
Evaluate tree-sitter extraction for structure-aware trace hints.
Evaluate GraphRAG/CodeQL/Joern extensions only after lexical baseline plateaus.
Evaluate embedding retrieval only with explicit regression-proof plan.

success_criteria: JSON plan is valid and committed in docs/planning.
Plan clearly separates MVP tasks from deferred tasks.
Muon DIS workflow tracing is explicitly named as first target.
Doxygen, tree-sitter, GraphRAG, CodeQL, and Joern are explicitly deferred.
MVP defines query packs, evidence capture, weekly reports, golden queries, and benchmark evaluation.
Plan enforces evidence-grounded claims and deterministic tests.

handoff.owner_profile: root-rag contributor focusing on retrieval QA and reproducibility
first_execution_steps: Draft Muon DIS query pack v0 and run against current index snapshot.
Implement evidence artifact emission and validate schema with tests.
Author golden query file and run initial benchmark baseline.
Publish week-1 tracing report with linked evidence artifacts.
handoff.review_cadence: Weekly benchmark + report review; tighten golden set and query packs incrementally.
handoff.done_definition_mvp: Muon DIS tracing loop runs locally end-to-end with deterministic artifacts, passing tests, and weekly evidence-grounded report output.
